scope_note: QA Pisano Period = Prime Splitting Criterion cert [415]. Primary sources: Wall, D.D. (1960) 'Fibonacci series modulo m' American Mathematical Monthly 67(6) doi:10.2307/2309169 (Pisano period pi(m): smallest k>0 with F_k=0 mod m and F_{k+1}=1 mod m; Wall's theorem: pi(p)|p-1 for p=+/-1 mod 5, pi(p)|2(p+1) for p=+/-2 mod 5, pi(5)=20); Ribenboim, P. (1996) 'The New Book of Prime Number Records' ISBN 978-0-387-94457-9 (Chapter on Fibonacci and Lucas sequences, prime splitting). CLAIM (narrow and falsifiable): The Pisano period pi(p) (period of the Fibonacci sequence mod p, computed by QA T-step iteration — pure integer arithmetic, Theorem NT) encodes the prime splitting class of p in Q(sqrt5)/Q: (C1) SPLIT (p%5 in {1,4}): pi(p) divides p-1; algebraic reason: p split => Z[phi]/p = F_p x F_p, Fibonacci recurrence splits into two independent F_p* recurrences, period divides |F_p*|=p-1 by Fermat; verified for 22 split primes p<=491; falsifiable: any split prime with pi(p) not dividing p-1 refutes; (C2) INERT (p%5 in {2,3}): pi(p) divides 2(p+1) AND pi(p) does NOT divide p-1; algebraic reason: p inert => Z[phi]/p = F_{p^2}, Frobenius has order 2, combined period divides 2|F_p*|=2(p+1) by Frobenius; non-divisibility of p-1 distinguishes inert from split; verified for 22 inert primes p<=193; (C3) RAMIFIED p=5: pi(5)=20=(p-1)*p=4*5; 20 does not divide p-1=4 and does not divide 2(p+1)=12; the ramified prime fits neither the split nor inert formula, consistent with Z[phi]/5 = F_5[x]/(x-3)^2 (repeated root, nilpotent structure); (C4) QA T-ORBIT CONNECTION: the QA T-step T(b,e)=(e,b+e) applied mod p to (0,1) generates the Fibonacci sequence mod p with period pi(p); verified for 7 sample primes (split 11,31,41; inert 2,3,7; ramified 5); the QA T-orbit period = Pisano period = prime class indicator; CHAIN: cert [133] derives the Eisenstein sign-flip identity from the QA T-step -> cert [414] shows this equals the Z[phi] norm form -> cert [415] shows the T-orbit period (Pisano period) encodes the prime splitting class predicted by the norm form. Together these three certs show: the QA discrete dynamics (T-step) encodes prime splitting in Q(sqrt5)/Q at three levels: (1) sign-flip identity = norm form algebraically, (2) representation by norm form = split/inert/ramified, (3) orbit period mod p = prime class. Each level is independently falsifiable by integer arithmetic. Derived 2026-06-14.
